name: SMTP2GO Email Delivery API
what_it_does: Managed SMTP relay and email delivery API — providing reliable email delivery via SMTP and REST API with global infrastructure, real-time analytics, bounce handling, and reputation management for transactional and bulk email senders.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails (confirmations, receipts, alerts) via SMTP relay or REST API
Replacing unreliable self-hosted SMTP servers with managed cloud delivery
Monitoring email delivery, opens, and bounces via reporting API
Sending from custom domains with SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration
Delivering emails globally with regional routing for optimal deliverability
not_for: Marketing automation (use Klaviyo or Braze — SMTP2GO is delivery infrastructure only)
Email template management without a separate ESP
Teams needing advanced segmentation, flows, or behavioral triggers
best_when: A developer needs reliable SMTP relay for application email with REST API fallback, real-time delivery tracking, and minimal setup friction.
avoid_when: You need email marketing campaigns, automation flows, or template management — SMTP2GO is pure delivery infrastructure.

auth.notes: API key from account dashboard. Passed as api_key in JSON body or header. SMTP uses username/password (email + API key). Account-scoped access. No fine-grained scopes. Separate subaccounts for multi-tenant use.

pricing.paid_tiers: From $15/month for 10,000 emails; higher volume plans available
pricing.notes: Generous free tier — 1,000 emails/month permanently. Australian company (founded NZ). Strong global deliverability infrastructure. Simple pricing per email. No credit card for free tier. Good for SMBs and developers who want reliability without complexity.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits by plan; 60-second injection limit on free

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Simple REST API for sending, managing suppressions, and querying delivery stats. SMTP relay as primary interface. Webhook for delivery events. API key in JSON body (not header) is non-standard. Good documentation with clear examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Email sends create new messages — not idempotent. Suppression list adds idempotent. Sending domain management idempotent. Stats queries always idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API key passed in JSON body (not Authorization header) — non-standard pattern
Domain verification required before sending — DNS records must propagate
Free tier rate-limited per 60 seconds — burst sending not supported
SMTP and API are dual interfaces — choose one for consistency in agent implementation

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US, EU, AU data residency. DKIM/SPF/DMARC support for sender authentication. API key auth. Non-standard body placement for API key slightly increases leak risk. No formal certifications beyond GDPR.
